open_obligations.what_is_open: Primitive, verifiable conditions for a genuine concrete two-fold nuisance-training algorithm that imply log max(K_{+,n},K_{-,n})=o(n C_star(delta_n)) along every stated admissible sequence remain unidentified.
open_obligations.obstruction: The maintained training-random L4 O_P(r_n) premise controls typical nuisance error only under the original hypotheses and supplies no exponential control under the Hellinger-tilted law m_n^n. Such O_P control permits vanishing-probability training events on which the returned functions have arbitrarily large amplitude. The rare-event nuisance perturbation in def:rare-event-nuisance-perturbation remains admissible under that premise but can reverse the score-variance sign with probability at least q_n^2 under P_{+,delta_n}^n. Since log(q_n^2)=-2 log log(n+e)=o(n C_star(delta_n)) under the definition's admissibility condition, this precludes certification of any positive Chernoff-error exponent from the L4 O_P premise alone. Conditioning on the joint returned-function sigma-field G_n does not repair the missing exponentially small tail control, because G_n records the contaminated returned functions rather than making their rare training events negligible at the required scale.
open_obligations.attempted: The tilted change-of-measure identities were used to reduce the desired exponent to control of K_{+,n} and K_{-,n}; ordinary cross-fitted L4 perturbation bounds and training-random O_P containment were then tested against the explicit rare-event perturbation. They yield sign consistency in the regime already covered by prop:score-contrast-template but do not yield exponential tilted-loss control. No concrete nuisance learner is specified in the frozen core, and the authoritative directive forbids replacing the open question with an oracle, tangent-aware, delta_n-aware, or orientation-aware trainer.
open_obligations.partial_result: The exact bookkeeping identities are P_{+,delta_n}^n(D_n=-)=exp(-n C_star(delta_n))K_{+,n} and P_{-,delta_n}^n(D_n=+)=exp(-n C_star(delta_n))K_{-,n}. Hence the requested condition is sufficient exactly when both tilted losses are exp(o(n C_star(delta_n))). Moreover, the frozen L4 premise is insufficient: for the admissible rare-event perturbation, P_{+,delta_n}^n(D_n=-)>=q_n^2, so log K_{+,n}>=n C_star(delta_n)+2 log q_n=n C_star(delta_n)-o(n C_star(delta_n)), which contradicts log K_{+,n}=o(n C_star(delta_n)). The strongest positive result available from the frozen primitives remains the conditional sign consistency in prop:score-contrast-template when r_n=o(sqrt(C_m(delta_n))).
